payload.title: LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD & METRO-NORTH RAILROAD KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
payload.description: Key performance metrics document prepared for the October 2025 meeting of the Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committees.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. Operates 700 trains daily and maintains 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.
Comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. Operates 700 trains daily and maintains 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.

Comprehensive upgrade at Hollis Station focusing on safety, passenger flow, and ADA accessibility including platform replacements and extensions to accommodate 8-car trains, ASIM platforms, new longer platform canopies, new elevator, ADA-compliant ramp, pedestrian tunnel from platform B and four additional staircases. A new pedestrian tunnel at the west end was installed on the weekend of November 1st.
18-month project to make St. Albans Station fully ADA accessible with new elevators. Ribbon cutting on November 13.
New additional train departing Penn Station at 3:20 PM and arriving at Ronkonkoma at 4:41 PM, added as of November 9 to keep up with demand on the Ronkonkoma Branch during the pre-PM Peak.

payload.project_name: West Hempstead Branch Track Infrastructure Upgrades
payload.description: Major strides in upgrading track infrastructure along the West Hempstead Branch since spring 2025. Key improvements included rehabilitation and repaving of Woodfield Road, Ocean Avenue, and Hempstead Avenue grade crossings, waterproofing and repairing the viaduct between Valley Stream and Westwood stations, and rebuilding 4.5 miles of track.

tool_args.label: Unified Trash Facility at Grand Central Terminal

payload.project_name: Unified Trash Facility at Grand Central Terminal
payload.description: Unified Trash Facility adjacent to Track No. 115 on GCT's lower level, completed October 31, 2025. Built by MTA Construction & Development partnered with John Civetta & Sons Inc. Jointly used by Metro-North and LIRR to transport waste to BN Yard in the Bronx. New equipment procured and 15 flat cars modified for trash transport.

Eighth locomotive rebranded as part of Metro-North's Heritage Series. Locomotive No. 216 honors the service and sacrifice of the nation's veterans. Design by Emily Moser, developed with input from Metro-North's veteran workforce. Unveiled November 10 at North White Plains shop.

Two Holiday Lights Trains operating from November 28 through New Year's Day. One electric multiple-unit set, one diesel-hauled set that can operate across entire Metro-North system. Both feature decorative holiday lights and special holiday-themed announcements. Operate on regularly scheduled trains on all three East of Hudson lines.
Ribbon cutting on the brand new St. Albans Station, the latest LIRR station to become fully ADA accessible.
Annual On-Track Safety Summit at Hillside Facility, hosted by LIRR, welcoming representatives from Metro-North, NJT, PATH, Keolis, Amtrak, and MBTA.
Annual Veterans Day Wreath Hanging Ceremony at Grand Central Terminal, attended by veterans, employees, commuters, and visitors.
Unveiling of Veterans Tribute Locomotive (Engine 216) at North White Plains shop.
Completion of construction of Unified Trash Facility adjacent to Track No. 115 on GCT's lower level.
New additional train departing Penn Station at 3:20 PM and arriving at Ronkonkoma at 4:41 PM began operation.
Total LIRR ridership in October 2025, a new post-COVID high

tool_args.label: Holiday Lights Project to Launch Event

payload.description: Holiday Lights Trains begin operation on November 28

National Escalator Safety Week campaign across eight stations: Babylon, Freeport, Hicksville, Baldwin, Massapequa Park, Merrick, Penn Station, and Grand Central Madison. Collaborated with NYCT, PATH, NJT, Grand Central Madison Operating Company, and Metro-North.
Successful installation of new pedestrian tunnel at the west end of Hollis Station, positioned beneath the active Hillside Yard Lead track.
Pedestrian tunnel installation milestone achieved during weekend of November 1 at Hollis Station
